content_class_declaration: canonical (consistent visual-language pass — codify the shared visual contract in the <PersonaPage> component; verify-and-codify disposition, no additive CSS)

step_1_capture_state.target_surface: site/src/components/sections/PersonaPage.astro (the shared primitive all 5 role pages wrap)
step_1_capture_state.method: audit the shared visual stack across all 5 pages — identify which layer owns section rhythm vs the opener vs single-use styles — to ensure the visual language is unified AND that unification is intentional/documented (not accidental)

visual_stack.section_rhythm_owner: .prose (site/src/styles/global.css) — defines h1-h4 margins+sizes, p margin+line-height 1.75, ul/ol margin+padding, li margin, pre, hr; applied to every role page via DocumentationLayout's <article class="doc-content prose">
visual_stack.opener_owner: .lede — owned in PersonaPage <style>; rendered via the named `lede` slot so the scoped class applies; scoped `.lede[data-astro-cid]` (0,2,0) out-specifies `.prose p` (0,1,1) so the opener styling holds
visual_stack.page_local: enterprise .eval-table only (single-use table; token-based margins/padding; correct to keep local per thin-wrapper contract)

pre_change_inventory.inconsistency_scan: NONE — all 5 pages share .prose rhythm + the now-centralized .lede; no page-local rule fights .prose; the visual language is ALREADY unified by .prose (global) + the S2 migration (.lede + jsonLD centralized)

step_2_persona_qa.method: agent-simulated 3-persona Q&A on whether/how to unify visual language across the 5 pages

q1_already_consistent.question: Do the 5 role pages already render in a consistent visual language, or is per-page divergence visible?
q1_already_consistent.answer: Already consistent. Heading/paragraph/list rhythm is set by the global `.prose` class (applied by DocumentationLayout to every doc page, not just these 5), and the page-opener `.lede` was centralized into <PersonaPage> in S2. The only page-local style is enterprise's single-use `.eval-table`. There is no per-page typography divergence to fix.

q2_right_change.question: Should this cycle add shared h2/ul/li rules to <PersonaPage> to 'unify' typography?
q2_right_change.answer: No — that would be a regression risk and a no-op at best. `.prose` already owns that rhythm; redeclaring it in the component would duplicate global rules, and because page bodies arrive through the DEFAULT slot, a component-scoped `h2`/`ul` rule wouldn't even reach them (mission Risk #7). The genuine, durable win is to CODIFY the visual contract in the component doc-comment so the next person who builds a role page inherits the unified look intentionally and doesn't re-add CSS that fights `.prose`.

q3_maintainability.question: What protects visual consistency when a 6th role page is added later?
q3_maintainability.answer: A documented contract on the primitive itself. With the layered ownership written into <PersonaPage> (`.prose` = rhythm, `.lede` = opener, single-use = local), a new page wraps the component, fills two slots, and is visually consistent by construction — no design review needed to keep parity. This is the same composition guarantee aDNA makes for modules->lattices.

step_3_decide.change_selected: VERIFY-AND-CODIFY — confirm the visual language is already unified (.prose + centralized .lede), then codify the layered visual contract in the <PersonaPage> doc-comment (rhythm owned by global .prose via DocumentationLayout's prose article; .lede owned here; single-use styles stay local). NO additive CSS — adding h2/ul/li rules would duplicate .prose and not reach slotted content (Risk #7).
step_3_decide.ceiling_declared: doc-comment codification of an existing shared contract; no rendered-output change (render parity trivially preserved)

step_4_implement.implementation_notes: Added a 'Visual contract' block to the component doc-comment enumerating the three ownership layers: (1) section rhythm owned by global `.prose` (site/src/styles/global.css) via DocumentationLayout's `<article class="doc-content prose">` — with an explicit 'do NOT redeclare h2/p/ul/li here' note citing both duplication and the default-slot scoped-style constraint; (2) `.lede` opener owned in the component; (3) single-use page styles stay local. No CSS added; no markup changed; render output unchanged.
lift_precedents_honored: codify-then-inherit (document the contract on the primitive so consumers inherit it)
self-reference (the primitive documents its own composition guarantee)
avoid_precedents_avoided: scoped rules that cannot reach default-slot content (Risk #7)

step_5_re_capture.method: npm run build + built-HTML spot-check (enterprise + researchers) for lede + TechArticle jsonLD presence

post_change_inventory.render_parity: PASS — comment-only change; rendered output unchanged. Spot-check: enterprise + researchers both still emit class="lede" + "@type":"TechArticle" jsonLD.
post_change_inventory.visual_language_status: unified + now documented (was already unified by .prose + centralized .lede; this cycle makes the contract explicit)

step_6_validate.method: 5-lens continuous-discipline overlay applied to the doc-comment prose added this cycle

five_lens.lens_1_word_count_outliers: pass — the Visual-contract block is tight (3 numbered layers, 1 closing sentence); no outlier verbosity
five_lens.lens_3_glossary_tightening: pass — uses established terms (`.prose`, default/named slot, scoped style) with no new jargon
five_lens.lens_4_clarity_checklist: pass — dual-audience: the 'why' (inherit the unified look for free) is plain; the technical specificity (duplication + Risk #7 default-slot constraint) is precise; one concept per layer

step_6_validate.parity_verdict: PASS — render-layer unchanged; visual language verified consistent across all 5 pages and now codified on the primitive

step_7_record.iii_result: Cycle 138 complete (canonical, verify-and-codify). The 5 role pages already share one visual language — section rhythm via the global `.prose` stylesheet (applied by DocumentationLayout), the `.lede` opener centralized into <PersonaPage> in S2 — so the correct visual-language pass is to CODIFY that contract, not add CSS. Added an 11-line 'Visual contract' block to the component doc-comment naming the three ownership layers (`.prose` = rhythm, `.lede` = opener, single-use = local) with an explicit 'do not redeclare .prose rules here' note (duplication + default-slot scoped-style constraint, Risk #7). Build steady 155 pages; render output unchanged. A 6th role page now inherits the unified look by construction. Honest finding for the AAR: the theme-set's 'unify visual language' expectation was largely pre-satisfied by `.prose` + the migration; the durable cycle-138 value is the documented contract.
step_7_record.self_reference_so8: the component now documents its own composition guarantee — five role pages collapse onto one primitive whose contract makes the next page consistent by construction, the same module->lattice composition discipline the vault teaches.
seeds: skill_visual_contract_codification (1/3) — NEW SEED: a visual-language cycle whose correct disposition, once a shared stylesheet + shared primitive already unify the look, is to document the ownership contract on the primitive rather than add redundant component CSS
skill_evaluation_only_disposition (reinforced) — 3rd canonical instance after M5.4 cycle 119 + M5.6 cycle 137; here the 'no additive CSS' half is evaluation-only (the codification is the change)
seed_estimate_drift_finding_for_d16 (note) — theme-set 'unify visual language / 150-270 ln pages' expectation was pre-satisfied post-D12 + by .prose; record for D16+ target-setting (mission Risk #4)
